claim: AI coding assistants like Claude Code lack deep human-AI collaboration on two key dimensions: curated knowledge bases and shared task management
actionable: Don't rely solely on AI assistants' web search or built-in task lists; use Archon or similar MCP tools to curate your own knowledge base and share task visibility with the AI
source_title: The OFFICIAL Archon Guide - 10x Your AI Coding Workflow

claim: Archon uses MCP (Model Context Protocol) to give AI coding assistants RAG access to curated documentation and real-time updates to shared task boards
actionable: Integrate Archon as an MCP server into your AI coding assistant (Claude Code, Windsurf, etc.) to give it access to your project's documentation and task queue
source_title: The OFFICIAL Archon Guide - 10x Your AI Coding Workflow

claim: Archon crawls documentation from library websites (especially .llm.txt and sitemap.xml formats) and indexes them for semantic and keyword RAG searches
actionable: When setting up Archon, prioritize crawling .llm.txt and sitemap.xml endpoints for library docs rather than raw web pages for better RAG performance
source_title: The OFFICIAL Archon Guide - 10x Your AI Coding Workflow

claim: Archon provides a kanban-style task board that the AI can update via MCP, allowing real-time synchronization of agent task progress visible to the human
actionable: Use Archon's task board to let your AI coding assistant break down implementation into discrete tasks and move them through a workflow (To Do → Doing → Review → Done)
source_title: The OFFICIAL Archon Guide - 10x Your AI Coding Workflow

claim: Archon requires Docker, Supabase (free tier available), and an LLM API key (OpenAI, Gemini, or local Ollama); setup takes 10-15 minutes via docker compose
actionable: Set up Archon locally for production-grade human-AI code collaboration by cloning the repo, configuring Supabase and LLM keys in .env, then running docker compose up
source_title: The OFFICIAL Archon Guide - 10x Your AI Coding Workflow

claim: Using markdown command files as reusable workflows (like /plan and /execute) lets you standardize how your AI coding assistant approaches planning and implementation across projects
actionable: Create markdown files defining /plan and /execute workflows; store them in your Archon global rules so your AI assistant follows consistent planning and execution patterns
source_title: The OFFICIAL Archon Guide - 10x Your AI Coding Workflow

claim: Read-only database connections to Archon's Supabase instance provide a security layer; combined with IP whitelisting and data expiration, they prevent accidental data leaks
actionable: Always configure read-only roles for any external connections to your AI tooling infrastructure; use Archon's connection timeout and data retention settings to auto-expire sensitive query results
source_title: The OFFICIAL Archon Guide - 10x Your AI Coding Workflow
